viable_jobs.title: INTERNSHIP - Ads & Analytics - Summer 2026
viable_jobs.description:
Advertising & Analytics Intern

We're looking for someone with a passion for making numbers work and getting results. The Advertising & Analytics Intern will learn the ropes of advertising, from client introductions to strategy to ad placement and reporting. They'll learn what works, what doesn't, and how to use data to drive results for real clients. This is a monthly stipend position.

Candidates must be located in the Philadelphia region as this role is hybrid and will require time onsite for training and mentoring as well as occasionally to support client events.

Responsibilities:
• Basic reporting duties - compiling reports on Sprout, pulling data and updating custom dashboards
• Place ads, conduct quality checks and ensure ads are pacing and running smoothly
• Build audiences, conduct keyword research and compile targeting assets for launch
• Attend bi-weekly ads/analytics training sessions to learn real world ads and analytics applications
• Assist in compiling benchmark reports, conducting industry research and analyzing trends in marketing data to help make an impact for clients

Requirements:

15 hours hybrid work per week and access to your own laptop computer.

Please apply with a cover letter and a resume. Timeline: May through August 2026.

Monthly stipend.

viable_jobs.title: Summer 2026 - Data Science and Innovation Intern
viable_jobs.description:
Responsibilities

We are seeking a 2026 summer intern to support our Learning Management and Support Services (LMSS) contract with our Intelligence Community client. At Noblis, our LMSS contract has numerous teams that support the educational and professional development for our client's workforce. In this project, the intern will join our Special Projects and Innovations team to identify, develop, and implement data science strategies for a variety of different teams, applications, and types of data. These tools will be leveraged together to achieve strategic goals and objectives and to align innovation efforts with the organization's mission.

Responsibilities:
• Learn the interdependence of cross-functional teams and implement data strategy using data science skills.
• Use data software tools to design, implement, and evaluate organizational improvements.
• Support data-related projects by identifying user needs, collecting data, performing data analysis & interpretation and visualization of both time series process data and manufacturing/operational data.
• Report insights and help clients to make decisions and find improvement opportunities.

Required Qualifications
• U.S. Citizenship
• GPA of 3.3 or higher
• Pursuing an undergraduate or graduate degree
• Problem solving, critical thinking, and organizational skills
• Ability to be a self-starter with demonstrated customer support and collaboration expertise
• Ability to work in a team-based environment
• Ability to improvise and adapt open-source software solutions in a network-restricted secure environment
• Experience with basic coding and data visualization skills such as: Python, R, JavaScript, Tableau
• Experience with databases and management tools such as: SQL, Access, PowerBI, MongoDB, Elastic Stack
• Ability and interest in obtaining a TS/SCI clearance including polygraph

Desired Qualifications
• TS/SCI clearance
• Experience with Geospatial Data Management
• Experience with advanced ML/data science techniques using PyTorch, TensorFlow, or scikit-learn
• Familiarity with training and fine-tuning AI models including large language models, agent-based systems, and retrieval augmented generation (RAG)
• Familiarity with Agile development workflows
• People skills that lend themselves to emotional intelligence and the human centered design process
• Consulting experience
• Briefing/Public Speaking Skills

Overview

Noblis and our wholly owned subsidiaries, Noblis ESI, and Noblis MSD tackle the nation's toughest problems and apply advanced solutions to our clients' most critical missions. We bring the best of scientific thought, management, and engineering expertise together in an environment of independence and objectivity to deliver enduring impact on federal missions. Noblis works with a wide range of government clients in the defense, intelligence and federal civil sectors. Learn more at Noblis -About Us

Why work at a Noblis company?

Our employees find greater meaning in their work and balance the other things in life that matter to them. Our people are our greatest asset. They are exceptionally skilled, knowledgeable, team-oriented, and mission-driven individuals who want to do work that matters and benefits the public. Noblis has won numerous workplace awards. Noblis maintains a drug-free workplace.
• Remote/hybrid status is subject to change based on Noblis and/or government requirements

Commitment to Non-Discrimination

All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, ethnicity, sex, age, national origin, religion, physical or mental disability, pregnancy/childbirth and related medical conditions, veteran or military status, or any other characteristics protected by applicable federal, state, or local law.

If reasonable accommodation is needed to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform essential job functions, and/or to receive other benefits and privileges of employment, please

viable_jobs.title: Intern, Data Science & Artificial Intelligence (Summer 2026)
viable_jobs.description:
Description

Intern, Data Science & Artificial Intelligence

2026Internship Dates: June1, 2026- August7, 2026

AArete is one of a kind when it comes to consulting firm culture.

As an Intern at AArete, you'll have the opportunity to directly impact both team and client objectives while fostering your personal growth. We seek organized, motivated individuals excited to join our experienced consulting team and serve clients across healthcare, financial services, retail, and other industries.

AArete Interns work closely with clients to understand critical issues and develop strategies for meaningful change. You'll leverage your analytical skills, entrepreneurial spirit, and commitment to excellence. We're looking for critical thinkers with exceptional communication skills who are eager to dive deep into business challenges, data analytics, and business case development to drive project success.

The Role

In this role, you'll demonstrate your analytical abilities and support the successful execution of project goals and deliverables. You'll have the chance to make a significant impact by addressing unique business challenges in both client and internal projects.

Work You'll Do

You will work on projectsin a variety of different areas within technology, including:

-Data Architecture & Engineering

- AI & Machine Learning

-Business Intelligence & Visualization

-Data Science & Analytics

-Application Development

What you'll gain
Professional and personal growth through a comprehensive internship program designed to develop your skills and career Direct impact on team and client objectives, allowing you to contribute meaningfully to real-world projects Hands-on experience with our experienced technology team, working on impactful projects across diverse industries Exposure to various sectors, including healthcare, financial services, and retail, broadening your industry knowledge Mentorship and guidance from experienced professionals in the field Networking opportunities with professionals and leaders in the industry Competitive compensation and valuable insights to kickstart your consulting career

Requirements

Education & Eligibility
Current undergraduate student entering either their 3rd year of a4-yeardegree program or in the 4th year of a 5-year program(Spring 2027 Grad) Minimum3.0cumulative GPA Must be legally authorized to work in the United States without the need for employer sponsorship Must be able to spend the summer in our downtown Chicago office, personal preference and circumstances will be taken into consideration for any remote candidates who are unable to be on site

Skills & Abilities
Hands-on experience with Agentic AI and Generative AI systems Familiarity with machine learning concepts such as classification, clustering, feature engineering, and model evaluation Understanding of generative AI tools and their applications in analytics and automation Knowledge in Agentic AI system using Strands SDK (or equivalent) and inferencing approaches Strong interest in pursuing a career in data science and analytics Demonstrated passion for continuous learning and staying current with emerging data science tools and technologies Ability to approach problem-solving with logical, analytical, and data-driven thinking Capable of understanding business objectives and using data to craft and deliver meaningful insights and solutions Proficient in Python, SQL, and data visualization tools (such as Power BI or Tableau), with the ability to further develop and apply these skills Works effectively both independently and collaboratively in project teams Excited to support Data Science team in developing innovative, data-driven solutions for clients

Preferred Requirements
Degree/Major in Computer Science, Statistics, or related field 3.3 or higher cumulative and major GPA Relevant work experience, including internships, summer positions, or part-time jobs Active involvement in university clubs or organizations related to consult

viable_jobs.title: Data Scientist Intern for Passport Demand Forecasting
viable_jobs.description:
Overview

LMI is seeking a Data Science Intern with a strong background in statistical and econometric analysis to support our passport demand forecasting activities with the Department of State Passport Bureau (CA/PPT). This role will play an important part in developing, testing, and refining models used to forecast multiple forms of passport demand across the organization. The ideal candidate combines rigorous analytical thinking with creativity, technical curiosity, and sound judgment.

LMI is a new breed of digital solutions provider dedicated to accelerating government impact with innovation and speed. Investing in technology and prototypes ahead of need, LMI brings commercial-grade platforms and mission-ready AI to federal agencies at commercial speed.

Leveraging our mission-ready technology and solutions, proven expertise in federal deployment, and strategic relationships, we enhance outcomes for the government, efficiently and effectively. With a focus on agility and collaboration, LMI serves the defense, space, healthcare, and energy sectors—helping agencies navigate complexity and outpace change. Headquartered in Tysons, Virginia, LMI is committed to delivering impactful results that strengthen missions and drive lasting value.

Responsibilities
• Rapidly prototype and iterate on new models, methodologies, and analytical libraries that rely on cutting edge statistical and other tools. Assess their strengths and limitations.
• Critically evaluate data sources, modeling assumptions, and results, identifying risks, biases, and sources of uncertainty.
• Actively monitor US passport demand; survey results; policy developments; travel, economic, geopolitical, and other trends; and creatively explore means of using these data to forecast impacts on passport demand.
• Translate complex analytical findings into clear, actionable insights for technical and non-technical stakeholders.
• Work independently to explore novel approaches while applying healthy skepticism and methodological discipline.
• Stay current with emerging technologies, tools, and best practices in forecasting, statistics, and data science.
• Explore customer needs and help identify ways to add value.

Qualifications
• Strong programming skills (e.g., Python, R, etc.)
• Foundational coursework or experience in statistics, econometrics, or data science
• Self‑motivated and driven to explore complex analytical challenges
• Interest in data analysis, modeling, and forecasting
• Demonstrated ability to think critically about models, data, and assumptions
• Ability to work independently while maintaining methodological rigor
• Effective communication skills to present insights to technical and non‑technical stakeholders
• Enrollment in or completion of a Master’s program in STEM, economics, finance, or other data‑related field

Desired Attributes
• Familiarity with statistical and machine‑learning frameworks (e.g., statsmodels, pandas, numpy, matplotlib, etc.)
• Experience applying time series analysis techniques (e.g., ARIMA, VAR) or exploring machine‑learning models for forecasting
• Comfortable rapidly prototyping, testing, and iterating on statistical models
• Creative yet pragmatic mindset; able to challenge assumptions while aligning with organizational goals
• Interest in exploring the intersection of policy, economics, and travel trends
• Familiarity with cloud environments like AWS (serverless technologies are a plus)

Other Information
• Interns will need to provide their own transportation to and from work and locate and arrange for their own housing.
• All intern positions at LMI are paid positions.

Applicants must meet eligibility requirements for a U.S. Government security clearance. Only U.S. Citizens are eligible for a security clearance. For this position, LMI will only consider applicants with security clearances or applicants who are eligible for security clearances, due to the nature of the work.

viable_jobs.title: 2026 IDS Summer Intern – Performance Reporting & Functional Excellence
viable_jobs.description:
:::Please note: This is a temporary contractor opportunity at Novartis :::

Novartis touches the lives of a tenth of the world’s population. Every role here, regardless of contract type, makes an impact on human life!

Novartis US partners with external managed service provider Magnit Global to offer engaging temporary contractor opportunities like this, to those who are looking for flexibility in their career while looking to gain new skills and experiences providing services to an inclusive global medicines company. Click here to learn more about Magnit www.magnitglobal.com

Ready to work with/through Magnit at Novartis? Please read on...

2026 IDS Summer Intern Performance Reporting & Functional Excellence

Graduation Year: May 2027 – May 2028 / Level: Pursuing a bachelor's degree

Job Summary

The Performance Reporting Intern will support the US Performance Reporting team in delivering high-quality reporting products, KPI standardization, and cross-functional insights across TAs and enterprise reporting initiatives. This role provides hands-on experience with data analysis, visualization, automation initiatives, Gen AI and exposure to IDS, DDIT, Commercial, CE, Medical and NPS functions.

Key Responsibilities
• Lead the development and delivery of performance reporting dashboards and analytics to support strategic decision-making across brands and functions (Customer Engagement, Patient Services, Medical, Market Access, Finance)
• Assist in consolidating, validating, and documenting Functional Excellence, Brand Performance, and Make-or-Break KPIs.
• Partner with Reporting Product Owners and Data Product Managers to support PRIME reporting convergence and UAT cycles.
• Participate in data quality reviews, metric definition alignment, and stakeholder feedback collection/change management.
• Support workflow mapping and documentation for reporting processes.
• Help with creating self-serve BI Platform
• Prototype conversational AI in KPI Library project to scale
• Prepare materials for leadership updates and reporting governance forums.

Required Qualifications
• Pursuing a Bachelor’s degree in Analytics, Business, Data Science, Marketing, Economics, or related field.
• Strong analytical mindset; ability to interpret data and identify trends.
• Proficient in data storytelling, and performance measurement.
• Ability to identify and enhance reporting processes to improve accuracy and efficiency
• Proficiency in SQL for data extraction and transformation.
• Strong communication and collaboration skills.

Preferred Qualifications
• Understanding of pharmaceutical/healthcare industry,
• Hands on experience on analytical tools (Python, Alteryx, Dataiku) and visualization platforms (PowerBI, Qliksense,Tellius,Power App)

What The Intern Will Gain
• Exposure to enterprise reporting ecosystem (PRIME, Power BI, KPI governance).
• Experience working with cross-functional partners across IDS, CE, Medical, and NPS teams.
• Opportunity to contribute to reporting products used by leadership.
• Mentorship and career development from the Performance Reporting team.

Location: East Hanover, NJ (Onsite)

Pay Rate: (W2 Only - based on Education and Experience)

Rising Sophomore / Junior $24.00/hour

Rising Senior $26.00/hour

Contract: 2-month

Health, dental, vision, 401k

Why Novartis:

Helping people with disease and their families takes more than innovative science. It takes a community of smart, passionate people like you. Collaborating, supporting and inspiring each other. Combining to achieve breakthroughs that change patients’ lives. Ready to create a brighter future together?

EEO Statement:

We do not discriminate in recruitment, hiring, training, promotion or other employment practices for reasons of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, marital or veteran status, disability, or any other legally protected st

viable_jobs.title: Finance and Operations Intern
viable_jobs.description:
Summer 2026: Finance and Operations Intern

Gladstone, NJ, USA

Precision AQ is seeking a highly motivated Finance & Operations Intern to support our Engagement Solutions business during Summer 2026. This internship is designed for an individual interested in developing real-world experience at the intersection of finance, operations and data analytics within a growing services organization. The intern will work closely with senior Finance & Operations leadership to help build/support key reports, dashboards, staffing models, and AI-enabled workflows that support decision-making across the business. This role offers meaningful ownership, exposure to executive-level analysis, and hands-on experience with modern analytics and automation tools.
Responsibilities

Data Analytics & Reporting
• Support the development and maintenance of dashboards and recurring reports related to financial performance, utilization, staffing, and operational health
• Assist with data validation, cleanup, and reconciliation across multiple data sources
• Help identify trends, anomalies, and opportunities within financial and operational datasets
• Contribute to the documentation of reporting logic, metrics definitions, and assumptions
• Assist in enhancing staffing, capacity, and hiring models used to support forecasting and workforce planning
• Analyze historical staffing and utilization data to inform future modeling assumptions
• Support scenario analysis related to growth, hiring timing, and resource mix

AI & Automation Enablement
• Support Engagement Solutions initiatives focused on AI implementation and automation
• Assist with testing, evaluating, and documenting AI-enabled tools or workflows
• Partner with team members to help translate business needs into data or AI-driven solutions

Cross-Functional Exposure & Learning
• Collaborate with Finance, Operations, Analytics, and Technology stakeholders across Precision AQ
• Participate in team meetings, working sessions, and project reviews
• Gain exposure to how data, finance, and operations work together to support client delivery and business growth
Qualifications

Required
• Undergraduate currently pursuing a bachelor’s degree in Finance, Economics, Business Analytics, Data Science, Operations, or a related field
• Strong analytical skills with interest in data-driven decision-making
• Comfort working with Excel and PowerPoint
• Curiosity, initiative, and willingness to learn new tools and concepts
• Strong attention to detail and ability to follow through on assigned work

Preferred (Not Required)
• Exposure to data visualization tools (e.g., Power BI, Tableau)
• Introductory experience with SQL, Python, or statistics coursework
• Interest in AI, automation, or applied analytics in a business setting
• Prior internship, project, or coursework involving data analysis or modeling

Precision is required by law in some states or cities to include a reasonable estimate of the compensation range for this role. This compensation range takes into account the wide range of factors that are considered in making compensation decisions including but not limited to: skill sets, experience and training, licensure and certifications, and other business and organizational needs. The disclosed range estimate has not been adjusted for the applicable geographic differential associated with the location at which the position may be filled.

Reasonable estimate of the current range

$20 - $20 USD

Any data provided as a part of this application will be stored in accordance with our Privacy Policy. For CA applicants, please also refer to our CA Privacy Notice.

Precision Medicine Group is an Equal Opportunity Employer. Employment decisions are made without regard to race, color, age, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, veteran status or other characteristics protected by law.
